stem: At a charity carnival, attendees win tokens worth a different number of points depending on the shape. One attendee won T triangle tokens and H hexagon tokens worth a total of 1,350 points. The equation 60T + 75H = 1,350 represents this situation. How many more points is a hexagon token worth than a triangle token?
rationale: The coefficient 60 is the points per triangle token; 75 is the points per hexagon token. The difference is 75 - 60 = 15. Choice A is correct.
